summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package ships a postinstall hook in package.json (line 35) and a scanner.js module that together collect host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.platform(), process.cwd(), `whoami` via execSync) and environment variables, then POST them via https.request to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The same hardcoded GitHub Pages C2 endpoint appears in both the lifecycle script and scanner.js, with filesystem enumeration (fs.existsSync over multiple paths) feeding the exfiltration. There is no legitimate reason for a 'wallet security checker' to beacon system info and env to a personal GitHub Pages site at install time; the package's stated purpose is a cover story. Two unambiguous fingerprints match: hardcoded C2 in install-time-reachable code, and host/env data collected via child_process+os and shipped over HTTPS to that C2.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
